role_summary: Joint oral history at the Library of Congress; this catalog page treats the combined transcript as a single embedding signature.
ai_reading: Cynthia and Fletcher Anderson's top semantic neighbor is Geraldine Crawford Bennett, Toni Breaux, and Willie Elliot Jenkins (entry 46, count 158); the broader cluster includes Richard Barry Sobol (#104), Charles McLaurin (#17), Peggy Jean Connor (#96). The catalog flags a -0.85 reading toward Nonviolence as Theology on the Nonviolence as Theology vs Armed Self-Defense axis as the most-distinctive structural reading.
biographical_paragraph: This joint oral history is held at the Library of Congress as entry 25 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. The LoC transcript carries a publication-block audit tier: known issues exist that prevent full publication-grade certification, and any specific biographical or quoted claims drawn from the transcript itself should be confirmed against external sources before citation. Independent biographical sourcing for an extended orientation paragraph is pending; the strongest signal currently available is the embedding pattern named in the AI's reading above.
